title: Skills & Experience
text:
Professional Experience

🐠 Marine Ecology Officer
The Nature Conservancy | Tanzania | May 2026 – Present
Leading fieldwork coordination, data collection protocol design, and community stakeholder engagement under the Pamoja Tuhifadhi Bahari Yetu project. Represents TNC’s Oceans Strategy in Tanzania and the Western Indian Ocean region; coordinates partners including IUCN, WWF, WCS, and Forum CC; oversees workplan delivery against EU–IUCN and TNC requirements across Mtwara and Unguja North.


🌊 Youth Sounding Board Member
European Union Commission — Tanzania | June 2024 – Present
Appointed to the EU Ambassador’s Youth Sounding Board, advising on youth participation and empowerment in the blue economy, human capital, employment, and governance in Tanzania.


📊 Ecological Data Manager
Mwambao Coastal Community Network | Tanzania | June 2023 – March 2026
Managed all ecological data across Mwambao’s monitoring network — designing collection protocols, leading quality control, coordinating field teams, conducting analysis across fisheries, coral reef, mangrove, and seagrass programmes, and producing annual funder reports. Trained 500+ community members and in-water monitors. Developed the organisation’s second fish catch monitoring protocol and 10+ standardised ecological survey forms.


📢 Communications Manager
Sustainable Ocean Alliance — Tanzania | June 2023 – 2024
Managed internal communications for SOA Tanzania — onboarding new members, mobilising participation in ocean conservation projects, and acting as a bridge between organisational leadership and the broader membership.


🔬 University Research Assistant
BIOEEL-TZ Project, University of Dar es Salaam | Tanzania | July 2021 – February 2023
Conducted fish biodiversity and environmental data collection and analysis for the anguillid eel research project. Facilitated community workshops and school outreach, presented findings at international conferences, and co-produced a scientific documentary on eel biodiversity in Tanzania — serving as narrator.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
This website serves as a portfolio and professional CV showcasing my work in marine science, ecological data management, and coastal conservation.

What You’ll Find Here

Home: Introduction and overview of my professional background
Projects: Detailed descriptions of my recent and ongoing projects in marine conservation and data science
CV: Complete curriculum vitae with education, experience, and skills
About: Information about this site and how to connect with me



Professional Background
I am a marine scientist with a strong passion for understanding and protecting coastal ecosystems. With expertise in ecological data management, statistical analysis, and conservation planning, I work to bridge the gap between scientific research and practical conservation implementation.
My work at Mwambao Coastal Community Network focuses on: - Biodiversity monitoring and assessment - Data-driven conservation planning - Community engagement and capacity building - Ecosystem resilience and climate adaptation


Technical Expertise
I am proficient in R programming, GIS analysis, and data visualization. I use these tools to transform raw ecological data into actionable insights that inform conservation decisions and support sustainable management of coastal resources.


Let’s Connect
I’m always interested in collaborating on conservation projects, discussing marine science, or exploring opportunities to apply data science in environmental contexts. Feel free to reach out through:

Email: lauriankaijage@gmail.com
GitHub: Laurian Kaijage
LinkedIn: Laurian Kaijage




About This Website
This portfolio website was built using Quarto, a modern publishing system for combining code, data, and narrative content. The source code is available on GitHub.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
What I Do


🌊 Field Research
Leading biodiversity surveys and ecological assessments across Tanzania’s coastline — from seagrass beds to coral reefs and mangrove forests.


📊 Data & Analysis
Turning field observations into actionable insights using R, GIS, and statistical modelling to guide conservation decisions.


🤝 Community Work
Building local capacity for conservation through training, engagement, and co-designing monitoring programs with coastal fishing communities.


🎬 Media & Advocacy
Participating in documentary productions and science communication to amplify conservation stories from the local coastal communities.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text: From the reef to the community — real outcomes on the Swahili Coast

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Work on the Ground
My conservation work spans 50 communities across Zanzibar and the Tanga region of Northern Tanzania. Every protocol I design, every dataset I analyse, and every person I train is part of a connected effort to put ecological evidence at the centre of coastal management decisions.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Key Impact Areas


👥 Community Training — Fisheries Data Collection
Trained 200 community members across 50 communities (Zanzibar and Tanga region) on fisheries data collection protocols covering reef fish, prawns, and octopus. Training equipped local fishers with the skills to systematically record catch and effort data, turning everyday fishing activity into a conservation monitoring tool.
Outcomes: - 200 trained community monitors actively collecting fisheries data - 50 communities covered across Zanzibar and Northern Tanzania - Community-generated data fed directly into local management plans and adaptive management decisions - Increased local ownership of fisheries resource management


🤿 In-Water Monitoring — Coral Reef Visual Census
Trained 300+ in-water monitors in underwater visual census (UVC) techniques for coral reef monitoring, including data collection and analysis. A landmark achievement: 70 of these monitors are women — breaking a significant cultural barrier in coastal Tanzania, where women’s participation in snorkelling and underwater activities is highly unconventional.
Outcomes: - 300+ trained underwater visual census monitors - 70 women trained as in-water monitors — unprecedented in this region - Training extended beyond data collection to include analysis of survey data - Results used to guide and support coral reef restoration efforts


🪸 Coral Reef Restored Area
Provided data analysis and monitoring support for the restoration of 347 hectares of coral reef restored area, including the deployment of 1,300 artificial reef structures. This work involved designing data collection protocols, coordinating field surveys, and analysing reef health data to track restoration progress.
Outcomes: - 347 ha of coral reef area restored and monitored - 1,300 artificial reef structures deployed and tracked - Reef health data used to measure restoration effectiveness - Monitoring protocols adapted for long-term community fish replenishment zone management


🌿 Mangrove Restoration & Seagrass Monitoring
Supported the restoration of approximately 60 hectares of mangrove habitat through data collection protocol design and analysis. Also led seagrass monitoring surveys contributing to understanding of coastal habitat connectivity and ecosystem health.
Outcomes: - 60 ha of mangrove area supported through data-driven restoration - Seagrass monitoring integrated into multi-habitat coastal assessment - Restoration progress tracked with standardised protocols - Data informing coastal ecosystem management decisions


📋 Protocols & Survey Tools
Developed Mwambao’s second fish catch monitoring data protocol — a landmark institutional tool that standardises fisheries data collection across the organisation’s entire network. Created 10+ ecological survey forms covering all major survey types used by the ecological department.
Outcomes: - New fish catch monitoring protocol in active use across the network - 10+ survey forms designed for reef fish, coral, seagrass, mangrove, and octopus monitoring - Standardised data collection reducing errors and improving comparability - Institutional data quality significantly improved


📊 Ecological Data Analysis
Led all ecological data analysis for Mwambao’s ecological department — spanning fisheries, mangrove restoration, seagrass monitoring, and coral reef monitoring. Transforming raw field data from across the network into findings that drive conservation planning and adaptive management.
Outcomes: - End-to-end analysis across four ecosystem monitoring programmes - Annual reports and internal dashboards produced for each programme - Data-driven adaptive management recommendations delivered to teams - Analytical pipelines built in R for reproducible, efficient reporting

text:
Professional Journey
A timeline of roles, projects, and milestones across Tanzania’s coastal conservation sector.

Supported international conference reporting and co-produced a scientific documentary on anguillid eels in Tanzania as narrator and field researcher.

      Served as Communications Manager for SOA Tanzania — mobilising members, facilitating onboarding, bridging leadership and community members, and promoting active participation in ocean conservation programmes.
    
  

  
    
      2026 – Present
      Marine Ecology Officer — The Nature Conservancy
      Joined The Nature Conservancy as Marine Ecology Officer, leading fieldwork coordination, data collection protocols, and stakeholder engagement under the Pamoja Tuhifadhi Bahari Yetu project — working across Mtwara and Unguja North to strengthen marine co-management and biodiversity conservation.

title: Projects
text: Science in action — funded projects driving conservation across Tanzania's coast

title: Projects
text:
Funded Projects


Pamoja Tuhifadhi Bahari Yetu
Funder: European Union | Organisation: The Nature Conservancy Period: May 2026 – Present
A multi-partner EU-funded project designed to strengthen governance frameworks, improve institutional capacity, promote community engagement, and support research, monitoring and surveillance systems for coastal and marine ecosystems in Tanzania. The overarching goal is to improve environmental protection and biodiversity conservation.
My Role: - Coordinate and lead fieldwork activities in Mtwara and Unguja North - Design data collection protocols and oversee field data management - Facilitate community and partner consultations (IUCN, WWF, WCS, Forum CC) - Track workplan delivery against EU–IUCN and TNC requirements - Coordinate fellows, interns, and consultants on programme objectives
Marine Conservation EU Funded Community Engagement Governance


TNC — Strengthening Marine Co-management & Livelihoods
Funder: The Nature Conservancy | Organisation: The Nature Conservancy Period: July 2025 – April 2026
Focused on strengthening fisheries co-management in Tanga and reef restoration in Pemba Island, Tanzania. The project supports sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities through improved marine resource governance and restoration of key habitats.
My Role: - Support fisheries co-management planning and community engagement - Coordinate reef restoration monitoring and data collection - Facilitate stakeholder engagement across Tanga and Pemba Island
Reef Restoration Co-management Livelihoods Pemba Island


Blue Action Fund — Socio-Ecological Resilience, Pemba Channel
Funder: Blue Action Fund | Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network Period: June 2023 – April 2026
Supported marine conservation projects aimed at increasing socio-ecological resilience through community-led management of key coastal ecosystems in the Pemba Channel, Tanzania. The programme focused on conserving the ocean and improving the livelihoods of coastal communities.
My Role: - Led ecological data management and analysis throughout the project lifecycle - Designed and implemented biodiversity monitoring protocols - Produced funder reports and contributed to adaptive management decisions - Trained community monitors in fisheries and reef health data collection
Blue Economy Community-Led Conservation Pemba Channel Resilience


NORAD — Sustainable & Inclusive Coastal Marine Economy
Funder: NORAD | Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network Period: June 2023 – June 2025
Through NORAD support, this project actively engaged communities in Mkokotoni, Mtowapwani, Muwanda, Makoba, Mafufuni, Misufini, Bima Subuni, Boma Kichakamiba, Zingibar, Mwaboza, and Moa towards building a sustainable and inclusive coastal marine economy on the East African Coast.
My Role: - Ecological data collection and analysis across community sites - Supported community marine economy planning with ecological evidence - Coordinated field teams and community-based data collectors
Marine Economy Community East Africa NORAD


ECOFISH — Sustainable Marine Resource Management in Small-Scale Fisheries
Funder: European Union | Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network Period: June 2023 – December 2024
Part of the EU-funded 6-year ECOFISH programme (2018–2024), this component promoted equitable governance of tenure in small-scale fishing communities in Zanzibar and the NE Tanzanian coast — extending successful octopus closure pilot initiatives and improving policies for better fisheries management across Eastern Africa, Southern Africa, and the Indian Ocean Region.
My Role: - Data collection and protocol design for octopus and fisheries monitoring - Supported community governance planning with catch and effort data - Contributed to regional data sharing and policy advocacy
Fisheries Governance Octopus Closures EU Funded Small-Scale Fisheries


BIOEEL-TZ — Anguillid Eel Biodiversity, Tanzania
Funder: International / University Partnership | Organisation: University of Dar es Salaam Period: March 2021 – January 2024
An interdisciplinary project bridging knowledge gaps on migratory anguillid eels in Tanzania — examining the impacts of fisheries and economic activities on eel populations. The project combined scientific data collection with public outreach, involving fishing communities and schools. A milestone for awareness-raising about unknown natural resources in Tanzania.
🔗 meerwissen.org/partnership-projects/bioeels-tz/
My Role: - Fish biodiversity data collection and environmental parameter analysis - Narrated and contributed to a scientific documentary on anguillid eels (part of MSc thesis) - Facilitated community workshops and school outreach activities - Presented findings at international conferences
Eels Biodiversity Documentary MSc Research Community Outreach

title: Documentaries & Media
text: Stories from the Swahili Coast — conservation through film and science communication

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Documentary Features
I have been involved in documentary productions both as a narrator and as a field director — bringing scientific research and conservation stories to wider audiences.


Anguillid Eels of Tanzania — Biodiversity & Conservation Awareness
Year: 2022 | Project: BIOEEL-TZ, University of Dar es Salaam
This documentary was produced as part of the BIOEEL-TZ research project and formed a component of my MSc thesis work. I served as narrator — translating the science of anguillid eel biodiversity into an accessible story for communities, schools, and the wider public. The film raised awareness about the ecological importance of eels as an underappreciated natural resource in Tanzania.

  


Anguillid Eels MSc Research Narrator Biodiversity Science Communication




Octopus Closures — Coastal Conservation in Action
Year: 2024 | Project: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Produced during my time at Mwambao, this documentary highlights octopus closure strategies as a conservation and livelihood tool for small-scale fishing communities. I directed the underwater video taking — coordinating underwater filming to capture reef habitats, marine life, and the field realities of community-based conservation. The film aims to build public awareness and support for community-led marine management strategies.

  


Octopus Closures Underwater Filming Community Conservation Mwambao Marine Livelihoods

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
In the Press
Articles and features where my work and perspective have been cited.


Whose Knowledge Counts? — African Ocean Forum (UNOC3)
Publication: AFO — UN Ocean Conference 3 Feature | Read article →
This piece examines the tension between high-tech ocean mapping initiatives and the traditional ecological knowledge held by coastal communities. My journey from data analyst to certified diver is highlighted as an example of why embodied, first-hand experience with marine ecosystems is irreplaceable in effective ocean governance.

“Having that first-hand look of how things are underwater really shaped my understanding of the whole ecosystem and how I can align my message to the communities properly.” — Laurian Kaijage, quoted in AFO UNOC3 feature

The article also notes my participation in the Tanzania DiveLab under AFO’s Ocean Access Program — creating equitable ocean access for marine scientists, storytellers, filmmakers, and marine rangers.



WIO Early Career Scientists Network — WIOMSA Blog
Publication: Western Indian Ocean Marine Science Association (WIOMSA) | Read feature →
Featured in the WIO Early Career Scientists Network (WIO-ECSN) updates, recognising my marine research and community engagement work at Mwambao Coastal Community Network and as Hub Leader at SOA Tanzania.

“Laurian’s work in marine research and community engagement has advanced local conservation initiatives and fostered sustainable practices among coastal populations.” — WIOMSA WIO-ECSN Update, 2024

Also noted: my appointment (alongside a fellow appointee) to advise the EU Ambassador to Tanzania and the East African Community on youth participation and empowerment in the blue economy, human capital, employment, and governance.



Diffusion of Innovation in Community-Managed Fisheries — Blue Ventures
Publication: Blue Ventures Explore | Read article →
Featured in Blue Ventures’ exploration of how communities collect and use fisheries data to manage resources and control fishing effort — showcasing Mwambao’s community-based monitoring approach.

“For the learning visit, we wanted to showcase how different communities collect data and use the same data to manage their fisheries and control fishing efforts.” — Kaijage Laurian, Mwambao Ecological Data Manager, quoted in Blue Ventures

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Science Communication
Beyond film, I communicate conservation science through:


📊 Technical Reports
Annual and project-specific biodiversity and restoration reports — translating field data into accessible findings for government, NGO, and community audiences across multiple funded programmes.


🗣️ Conferences & Presentations
Presented scientific findings at international conferences and school-level outreach sessions as part of the BIOEEL-TZ project — bridging academic research and public understanding.


👥 Community Workshops
Facilitated stakeholder and community workshops on fisheries governance, octopus closures, and biodiversity monitoring — building conservation ownership at the grassroots level.




  Open to documentary collaborations and science communication opportunities that amplify coastal conservation stories.
